Title: The NAC transcription factor FaRIF controls fruit ripening in strawberry

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: FvH4_3g20700 (orthologous F. vesca ID for FaNAC035/FaRIF)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; FaEXP2 fruit ripening/receptacle-specific promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in receptacles and also high in achenes; strongly induced from green to red ripening stages
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in receptacles during ripening, detectable at green stage and strongly increased through white/turning/red; also high in achenes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Anthocyanins; sucrose; ABA; volatile phenylpropanoids/terpenoids; lignin
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Flavonoid; carbohydrate; phytohormone; phenylpropanoid/terpenoid volatile; lignin-related phenylpropanoid
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins confer red fruit color and nutritional quality; sucrose contributes sweetness/soluble solids; ABA promotes ripening; volatiles contribute aroma; lignin contributes firmness
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit receptacle and achenes (anthocyanin/color traits); receptacle (sugars, ABA, aroma-related effects); vasculature and achenes for lignin staining
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fruit ripening-associated secondary metabolism, sugar accumulation, ABA biosynthesis/signaling, volatile biosynthesis, cell-wall remodeling, and aerobic/anaerobic energy metabolism balance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Primarily transcriptional activation of ripening programs; repression of monolignol/lignification branch; likely indirect network regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FaNCED3; FaZEP; FaNAC042; FaEOBII; FaDOF2; FaSPT; FaPRE1; FaEGS2; FaNES1; FaPL3; FaPL4; FaGH9B15; FaADPG2; FaEXP1; FaEXP2; FaEXP3; FaPME39; PAL1; PAL2; C4H; 4CL2; CHS1; FaSPS1; FaSUS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of FaNCED3, FaZEP, many cell-wall degradation/modification genes, flavonoid/anthocyanin genes, FaEOBII/FaDOF2/FaEGS2/FaNES1, and FaSPS1; represses monolignol-pathway genes and lignification; FaSUS1 and several glycolysis/fermentation genes become elevated when FaRIF is silenced
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ripening developmental cues; ABA positively regulates FaRIF expression according to cited inhibitor evidence
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop with ABA is proposed: FaRIF promotes ABA biosynthesis, and ABA positively regulates FaRIF expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; exogenous ABA infiltration of green receptacles
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strawberry fruit ripening, anthocyanin/pigment accumulation, sugar accumulation, softening, aroma-related gene expression, and ABA accumulation; represses excessive lignification; silencing delays ripening and extends fruit firmness/shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator of nonclimacteric strawberry fruit ripening and a major switch controlling multiple quality-related metabolites and processes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable RNAi silencing under 35S and FaEXP2 promoters; stable overexpression (35Spro:RIF and 35Spro:RIF-GFP); RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; fruit phenotyping; RNA-seq; metabolomics (UPLC-MS/MS and GC-MS/GC-TOF-MS); ABA quantification; exogenous ABA rescue; lignin staining
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaRIF is a ripening-induced NAC TF highly expressed in strawberry receptacles and achenes. 2. Stable silencing delays ripening, reduces red coloration, lowers ABA and soluble solids, increases firmness and lignification, and alters primary/secondary metabolism. 3. Overexpression accelerates color development and softening, confirming FaRIF as a central positive regulator of ripening-associated nutrient metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GEO: GSE167107); primary metabolomics by GC-MS; secondary metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS; ABA profiling by GC-TOF-MS
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression impairs plant development; constitutive silencing reduces soluble solids/sucrose despite improved firmness and delayed senescence; timing/tissue specificity is important for deployment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for editing or fine-tuning to improve strawberry shelf life and firmness. Late/tissue-specific downregulation may preserve sugar content better than constitutive silencing; useful for balancing firmness, ripening rate, color, aroma, and postharvest life
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally identified as FaNAC035 and renamed Ripening Inducing Factor (FaRIF). Receptacle-specific RNAi reproduced firmness/color effects without SSC reduction, suggesting stage-specific control of sugar accumulation from earlier development.
